channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Hasso Plattner sums up what his expectations are for the Sharks this season
item.description: NHL: After finishing in last place in back-to-back seasons, San Jose Sharks need to improve in 2025-26
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: Michigan football’s defense, USC’s offense to decide key Big Ten showdown in historic Coliseum clash
item.description: The No. 15-ranked Michigan Wolverines face the USC Trojans in a highly anticipated matchup at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carr, Local 4

item.title: Valentin Vacherot stuns Novak Djokovic and could face cousin in Shanghai Masters final
item.description: Valentin Vacherot earned the biggest win of his career by beating a hobbled Novak Djokovic 6-3, 6-4 on Saturday to reach the Shanghai Masters final — where he could face his cousin.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Monday offers Tyrique Stevenson an opportunity for redemption, one year after the play critics use to define him
item.description: Tyrique Stevenson has used the criticism he received from the "Fail Mary" play to become a better player, and has since earned Pro Football Focus's highest defensive grades among corners in Week 4.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Tarik Skubal turns in a dominant performance in Detroit's Game 5 loss to Mariners
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Tarik Skubal was terrific. And he was long gone when the season ended for the Detroit Tigers. Skubal struck out 13 in six dominant innings in Game 5 of the AL Division Series at Seattle on Friday night. But the Tigers were eliminated when Jorge Polanco singled home J.P.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Watch every touchdown in the battle of unbeatens as Carlsbad takes on Mission Hills
item.description: Carlsbad Lancers defeated Mission Hills Grizzlies 31-17 in a battle of two undefeated teams, with Eli MacNeal throwing four touchdowns and Darian Webb gaining huge chunks of yardage in the fourth quarter.
item.dc:creator: Sharisse Cohee, Fox 5

item.title: Mark Sanchez seen bloody, wounded in surveillance video after stabbing
item.description: Former NFL quarterback Mark Sanchez was seen bloody and wounded in new surveillance video obtained by TMZ.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: No. 24 South Florida scores 4 TDs in a 3 1/2-minute span and hands North Texas its 1st loss
item.description: Byrum Brown accounted for five touchdowns, Jhalyn Shuler returned a fumble 34 yards for South Florida's fourth TD in span of 3 1/2 minutes and the 24th-ranked Bulls beat North Texas 63-36.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Hawkins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Phoenix Mercury coach Nate Tibbetts ejected from WNBA Finals Game 4 after heated argument
item.description: Phoenix Mercury coach Nate Tibbetts was ejected from Game 4 of the WNBA Finals after receiving two quick technical fouls in the third quarter against the Las Vegas Aces.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Padres' Iglesias suspended 1 game, Bogaerts fined for dispute with umpires after season-ending loss
item.description: San Diego Padres infielder Jose Iglesias has been suspended for one game and fined for unprofessional conduct toward the umpires during the final game of the club’s Wild Card Series against the Cubs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Shedeur Sanders promoted to Browns backup QB, will be behind rookie Dillon Gabriel against Steelers
item.description: BEREA, Ohio (AP) — Shedeur Sanders has waited patiently for his chance to play for Cleveland. He's real close.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Phillies face an uncertain future following their 4th straight playoff exit
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Orion Kerkering airmailed a throw into the Los Angeles night that not only unimaginably ended the Phillies’ season — it all but ended the run of the core of a roster that suffered through four straight postseasons without a World Series title.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beards are back for the New York Islanders after old-school executive Lou Lamoriello's departure
item.description: EAST MEADOW, N.Y. (AP) — Images from the New York Islanders’ annual charity golf outing last month on social media went viral because something was very different about some of the familiar faces fans have seen for years. Many had beards on them.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jonathan Toews returns to NHL with hometown Jets after being out 2 seasons with long COVID
item.description: WINNIPEG, Manitoba (AP) — The spotlight was on Jonathan Toews when the Winnipeg Jets skated onto the ice for their season opener against Dallas on Thursday night. The Winnipeg-born Toews received a loud, long cheer from the sold-out crowd when he was introduced before the Jets' 5-4 loss.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yankees fan throws can at Vladimir Guerrero Jr's family before elimination: report
item.description: Toronto Blue Jays star Vladimir Guerrero Jr.'s family members were reportedly attacked with bottles by New York Yankees fans during the team's playoff elimination game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Celtics star Paul Pierce found asleep in car, arrested on suspicion of DUI
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former NBA player Paul Pierce was arrested Tuesday night on suspicion of driving under the influence on a Los Angeles highway after he was found asleep behind the wheel, state police said. California Highway Patrol officers responded at about 10:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clemson's Howard's Rock, 'Play Like a Champion Today' signs among beloved totems in college football
item.description: CLEMSON, S.C. (AP) — Hunter Renfrow snagged the game-winning touchdown in the College Football Playoff national championship game, was selected in the fifth round of the NFL draft and even made the Pro Bowl.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Parity or poor football? NFL is providing plenty of close games with exciting finishes
item.description: Some call it parity. Others consider it bad football. No matter how it’s viewed, the NFL is providing plenty of close games, thrilling comebacks, big upsets and late-minute heroics. It keeps viewers entertained — or at least tuned in until the end.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ian Happ's homer sets the tone for the Cubs in a playoff victory against the Brewers
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — With one big swing, Ian Happ sent a charge through an electric crowd of 41,770 at Wrigley Field. After a rough start to the playoffs, it was a pretty sweet moment for the longest-tenured player with the Chicago Cubs.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lakers' LeBron James sidelined for multiple weeks with sciatica, putting opener status in question
item.description: LeBron James will sit out for multiple weeks as he deals with sciatica, a pain that impacts the sciatic nerve. The four-time NBA champions availability for opening night is unclear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voice of the Tampa Bay Buccaneers shares his favorite game call
item.description: If you're a Bucs fan, chances are Gene Deckerhoff has graced the airwaves as the play-by-play announcer for the Tampa Bay Buccaneers.
item.dc:creator: Will Richardson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dodgers reliever Tanner Scott removed from NLDS roster due to illness
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Reliever Tanner Scott has been removed from the Los Angeles Dodgers' roster for the remainder of the NL Division Series because of illness. Scott was replaced by fellow left-handed reliever Justin Wrobleski.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Past, present & future: 50 years of Buccaneers football
item.description: There's a saying: teamwork makes the dream work. And countless people have poured into the Tampa Bay Buccaneers to make the franchise as successful as it is today.
item.dc:creator: Will Richardson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mark Sanchez case 'rubs me the wrong way:' Attorney Jose Baez
item.description: Celebrity attorney Jose Baez said that the drug test results requested by police will play a pivotal role in the Sanchez case.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: NFL fines Jerry Jones $250,000 for obscene MetLife gesture he says was inadvertent
item.description: FRISCO, Texas (AP) — The NFL said Tuesday it has fined Jerry Jones $250,000 for an obscene gesture toward fans at MetLife Stadium that the Dallas owner said was “inadvertent” and intended for fans of the Cowboys, not the New York Jets. The incident came late in Dallas' 37-22 victory on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NFL fines Jerry Jones $250K for 'obscene' gesture during Jets game
item.description: Videos circulated on social media Sunday of Jones flashing a middle finger at fans in fourth quarter of the Cowboys' 37-22 win over the Jets.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa Bay Rays new owners top priority: a new stadium
item.description: The new owners of the Tampa Bay Rays laid out some of their plans for the future of the team, but did not reveal many details.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Broncos rally from 14-point deficit to hand Eagles their first loss of the season, 21-17
item.description: Bo Nix led the Broncos on three scoring drives on three possessions in the fourth quarter, J.K. Dobbins rushed for a 2-yard score in the period and the Broncos wiped out a 14-point deficit to hand the Philadelphia Eagles their first loss of the season, 21-17.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-NFL quarterback Mark Sanchez stabbed multiple times in altercation leading to charges against him
item.description: Former NFL quarterback Mark Sanchez was pepper-sprayed and stabbed multiple times during a late-night altercation with a 69-year-old truck driver in a downtown Indianapolis alley, which resulted in criminal charges against the Fox Sports analyst, according to court records filed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and MIKE MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Noem: NFL ‘won’t be able to sleep at night’ over Bad Bunny Super Bowl performance
item.description: (The Hill) -- Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem said the NFL will not be “able to sleep at night” over its decision to pick Puerto Rican superstar Bad Bunny as the 2026 halftime performer.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Fire take win streak into game against Toronto
item.description: Toronto FC (5-13-13, 11th in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Fire (15-11-6, seventh in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 7:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
